ℹ Analysis Purpose & Scope

This report maps AD DC-to-DC traffic flows to the firewalls processing them. The objective is to identify every firewall in the traffic path and confirm whether its configuration is in the CCD Platform collection estate. Where configurations are missing, this is a data collection gap — not a security concern. All traffic observed is expected AD replication and trust traffic between registered DCs.

⚠ Config Collection Gap — 6 Firewalls

Six firewalls appear as dvc in Splunk flow logs but their configurations have not been collected into the CCD Platform estate. These are all confirmed Aetna-heritage devices:

4 × Aetna Affiliate FWs — 10.46.193.15 / .49 / .55 / .57 — confirmed per Aetna-Affiliate-FW.txt. Subnet 10.46.0.0/16 = CVTY DataCenter Phoenix per Panorama address table. Processing affiliate domain AD replication (pdchs.local, pdcss.local, ad.meritain.com, xauth.cofinity.net) between Coventry Phoenix DC and Las Vegas Switch COLO.

2 × Aetna WAN Hub FWs — 10.108.100.11 / .12 — HA pair at Edwardsville IL. Confirmed firewall infrastructure per Panorama table (10.108.0.0/16 = Firewall Networks). Processing HCB corporate traffic from Denver and LV COLO to MDC/WDC.

Action required: Obtain configs from Aetna network team and add to FW_CONFIGS collection for next rebuild.

Las Vegas Switch COLO — Affiliate AD Infrastructure

The Las Vegas Switch COLO hosts AD DC infrastructure for multiple Aetna affiliates, served by lgs1501-01b-0105-ext-dmz-pa-fw01 (zone: DMZ_pdc). Replication traffic to/from affiliate DCs in Coventry Phoenix DC (10.46.x.x) transits the Aetna affiliate FWs at 10.46.193.x whose configs are not yet collected.
